items.title: Rakuten Kobo rejects 45% of submissions after slew of AI-generated books uploaded
items.excerpt: E-book platform Rakuten Kobo was forced to reject 45% of submissions to its self-publishing platform last year, after a flood of AI-generated books were uploaded, prompting tighter moderation of Kobo Writing Life.

items.title: From IP Anxiety to Growth Strategy: The AI Conversation Publishing Needs to Have
items.excerpt: AI is not another incremental shift: it touches every part of what publishers do, from creation and curation to production, translation, marketing and rights management. The piece argues the industry must move from IP anxiety toward an AI growth strategy.

items.title: Commonwealth Foundation Clears Writers Accused of Using AI
items.excerpt: Three writers accused of using AI to create their Commonwealth Short Story Prize-winning pieces have been cleared after a review in which all five regional winners supplied drafts, time-stamped documents and notes.

items.title: Do You Want an AI Chatbot in Your Book? Google is Betting On "Yes"
items.excerpt: Google Play Books has launched Book Insights, a Gemini-built reading companion that offers spoiler-free recaps and lets readers ask questions while reading select titles, raising fresh questions about AI inside the reading experience.

items.title: Beijing International Book Fair Highlights Digital Publishing, Cross-Media Adaptations
items.excerpt: At the 40th Beijing International Book Fair (June 17-21), digital publishing, AI and cross-media adaptation dominated forums and special exhibits as Chinese publishers leaned into AI-driven transformation and reading.
